records.ety: [OE. ekename surname, hence, a nickname, an ekename being understood as a nekename, influenced also by E. nick, v. See Eke, and Name.]

defs: A name given in affectionate familiarity, sportive familiarity, contempt, or derision; a familiar or an opprobrious appellation; as, Nicholas's nickname is Nick.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Nicknamed (#); p. pr. & vb. n. Nicknaming.]

defs: To give a nickname to; to call by a nickname.
quotes: You nickname virtue; vice you should have spoke.
I altogether disclaim what has been nicknamed the doctrine of finality.
